OpenZeppelin Cairo Contracts 2025 Internal Review

Repository: github.com/OpenZeppelin/cairo-contracts

This document covers the openzeppelin-cairo component library used by StarkNet
account contracts.

## H-01 multicall isolation bypass in account component allows reentrancy

Severity High

The account component re-uses `tx_info.account_contract_address` across nested
calls inside the multicall executor. A malicious callee can call back into
`execute_calls` and observe sibling-call storage writes, defeating multicall
isolation.

Recommendation: guard the multicall executor with the OpenZeppelin
ReentrancyGuard Cairo component (reentrancyguard_cpt) and re-derive
tx_info per nested call.

## M-02 paymaster signature replay across outside_execution windows

Severity Medium

The paymaster nonce is checked but the SNIP-9 outside_execution timestamp
window is not bound to the chain_id. An attacker who observes a paymaster
signature on testnet can replay it on mainnet within the validity window.

Mitigation: include chain_id, contract_address, and a strictly-increasing
nonce inside the Pedersen hash that the StarkCurve signature commits to.
